title: Verify Whether a Tipper Is Left or Right From the Operator Perspective
summary: Use the manual's operator-perspective orientation rule to identify whether a tipper is left or right and confirm that the opposite-side assembly should be interpreted as a mirror image of the documented reference assembly.
when_to_use: Use when identifying a tipper as left or right, comparing a physical tipper to the manual diagram, or interpreting whether opposite-side assemblies should match as mirrored versions from the operator perspective.
not_for: Do not use this runbook for installation, adjustment, repair, or corrective maintenance because the source only provides orientation and mirror-image reference information.
Do not use this runbook to infer component differences between left and right tippers beyond the source statement that they are identical assemblies mirrored from the operator perspective.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Apply the manual's operator-perspective naming convention, compare the observed assembly to the documented left tipper reference, and document the orientation finding.
Provide access to the operator-facing tipper location or confirm the operator-facing viewpoint when needed.

safety_notes: This source packet supports a visual/reference procedure only and does not provide maintenance, motion, or lockout instructions.
Escalate if the operator perspective needed to assign left versus right cannot be established from the site setup.
Escalate if the physical assembly appears inconsistent with the documented mirror-image relationship.
access_or_tools_needed: Physical access or visual access to the tipper location

access_required: Physical access or visual access to the tipper location
system_preconditions: The observer can determine the tipper from the operator perspective referenced by the manual.

visual_references.description: Figure 3-4 reference diagram labeled 'Tipper Components (Left Tipper Shown)' used to compare component placement and mirror-image arrangement.
Operator station reference image that helps establish the operator-facing viewpoint.

steps.title: Establish the operator perspective at the tipper
steps.purpose: Ensure left/right naming is assigned using the same viewpoint defined by the manual.
steps.instruction: Go to the tipper location or obtain a clear visual of it and identify the assembly from the perspective of the operator, because the manual states that right and left tippers are based on the operator perspective.
steps.expected_result: The observer has established the operator-facing viewpoint to use for orientation naming.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator perspective is clear and can be consistently applied to the tipper being reviewed.
steps.failure_condition: The site setup or available view does not allow the operator perspective to be established confidently.

actions.label: Identify operator-facing viewpoint
actions.exact_action: Stand at the tipper location and identify the assembly from the operator perspective as stated in the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the operator station view to understand the operator-facing side of the equipment.

stop_or_escalate_if: The operator perspective needed to assign left versus right cannot be established from the site setup.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Right tippers and left tippers are based on the view from the perspective of the operator. The tippers are identical but assembled as mirror images of each other.

steps.title: Assign left or right tipper orientation
steps.purpose: Apply the documented naming convention to the observed tipper.
steps.instruction: Using the operator-perspective naming convention from the manual, determine whether the unit should be referred to as a left tipper or a right tipper.
steps.expected_result: The tipper is identified as left or right using the documented convention.
steps.healthy_condition: The orientation label is assigned consistently from the operator perspective.
steps.failure_condition: The orientation cannot be assigned confidently because the operator perspective remains unclear or conflicting.

actions.label: Determine tipper side
actions.exact_action: Determine whether the unit should be referred to as a left tipper or right tipper using that operator-perspective naming convention.

stop_or_escalate_if: The operator perspective remains unclear after review.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Right tippers and left tippers are based on the view from the perspective of the operator. The tippers are identical but assembled as mirror images of each other.

steps.title: Use Figure 3-4 as the left tipper reference
steps.purpose: Anchor the comparison to the manual's documented left tipper diagram.
steps.instruction: Use Figure 3-4, labeled 'Tipper Components (Left Tipper Shown),' as the documented reference for component placement when reviewing the assembly.
steps.expected_result: The left tipper reference diagram is selected as the baseline for comparison.
steps.healthy_condition: Figure 3-4 is available and clearly identified as the left tipper reference.
steps.failure_condition: The reference figure is unavailable or cannot be matched to the current review.

actions.label: Reference left tipper diagram
actions.exact_action: Use the left tipper diagram in Figure 3-4 as the documented reference for component placement.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Confirm the figure title 'Tipper Components (Left Tipper Shown)' and use the labeled component layout as the reference arrangement.

stop_or_escalate_if: The required Figure 3-4 reference is not available for comparison.

steps.title: Verify the opposite-side assembly as a mirror image
steps.purpose: Confirm that left and right tippers are interpreted as the same assembly mirrored rather than different designs.
steps.instruction: When comparing the opposite-side assembly, verify that it contains the same documented components arranged as a mirror image of the reference assembly rather than as a different design.
steps.expected_result: The opposite-side tipper is interpreted as the same assembly mirrored from the operator perspective.
steps.healthy_condition: The opposite-side assembly matches the documented mirror-image relationship described by the manual.
steps.failure_condition: The physical assembly appears inconsistent with the documented mirror-image relationship.

actions.label: Compare mirrored arrangement
actions.exact_action: When comparing the opposite-side assembly, verify that it contains the same documented components arranged as a mirror image rather than as a different design.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Compare labeled components and their relative placement to determine whether the opposite side is a mirrored arrangement of the left tipper reference.

stop_or_escalate_if: The physical assembly appears inconsistent with the documented mirror-image relationship.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Right tippers and left tippers are based on the view from the perspective of the operator. The tippers are identical but assembled as mirror images of each other.

steps.title: Record the orientation finding
steps.purpose: Capture the orientation decision and the documented interpretation for future reference.
steps.instruction: Record the tipper orientation and note that left and right tippers are identical assemblies mirrored from the operator perspective.
steps.expected_result: The orientation is documented together with the mirror-image interpretation.
steps.healthy_condition: The record clearly states whether the tipper is left or right and notes the documented mirror-image relationship.
steps.failure_condition: The orientation cannot be documented clearly because the viewpoint or mirror-image comparison remains unresolved.

actions.label: Document orientation result
actions.exact_action: Record the tipper orientation and note that left and right tippers are identical assemblies mirrored from the operator perspective.

stop_or_escalate_if: The orientation finding cannot be documented clearly due to unresolved viewpoint or comparison issues.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Right tippers and left tippers are based on the view from the perspective of the operator. The tippers are identical but assembled as mirror images of each other.

success_criteria: The tipper is consistently identified as left or right using the operator perspective.
Figure 3-4 is used as the documented left tipper reference.
The opposite-side assembly is interpreted as a mirror image of the documented reference rather than a different design.
The orientation finding is recorded with the mirror-image note.
healthy_conditions: The observed tipper can be labeled left or right using the manual's naming rule.
The opposite-side assembly matches the documented mirror-image relationship.
The left tipper reference figure is available for comparison.
failure_conditions: The operator perspective cannot be established from the site setup.
The physical assembly appears inconsistent with the documented mirror-image relationship.
The reference figure needed for comparison is unavailable.
The orientation finding cannot be documented clearly.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the physical assembly appears inconsistent with the documented mirror-image relationship.
Escalate if the operator perspective needed to assign left versus right cannot be established from the site setup.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Primary source figure for left tipper component placement and mirror-image comparison.
Reference image to help establish the operator-facing viewpoint.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Right tippers and left tippers are based on the view from the perspective of the operator. The tippers are identical but assembled as mirror images of each other.

missing_details: The source does not provide a detailed method for physically determining left versus right beyond stating that naming is based on the operator perspective.
The source does not define a required documentation system or record format for capturing the orientation result.
The source does not provide corrective actions if the assembly does not match the documented mirror-image relationship.
The source section text content was not included in the packet beyond referenced summaries and figure metadata.
